message:
Add RFC 6330 (RaptorQ Forward Error Correction) reference document

Add the full text of RFC 6330, "RaptorQ Forward Error Correction
Scheme for Object Delivery" (IETF Standards Track, August 2011,
3,867 lines) to docs/ as a local reference.

This RFC specifies the RaptorQ fountain code that underpins
FrankenSQLite's information-theoretic architecture. RaptorQ is a
rateless erasure code: given k source symbols, the encoder produces
a practically unlimited stream of encoding symbols, and any k'
symbols (where k' is only slightly larger than k) suffice for
complete reconstruction. Key properties exploited by FrankenSQLite:

- Self-healing WAL: WAL frames carry RaptorQ repair symbols,
  allowing recovery from sector-level corruption without full-page
  redundancy or double-write buffers
- Fountain-coded replication: snapshot transfer where receivers
  reconstruct from any sufficient subset of received symbols,
  tolerating arbitrary packet loss without retransmission
- Version chain compression: MVCC page deltas encoded as RaptorQ
  source blocks, reducing memory overhead for long-running readers
- Algebraic write merge: non-conflicting concurrent page writes
  merged via linear algebra over GF(256), reducing false conflicts
  in the MVCC page lock table

The document is authored by M. Luby, A. Shokrollahi, M. Watson,
T. Stockhammer, and L. Minder. FrankenSQLite uses asupersync's
production-grade RaptorQ implementation rather than implementing
the codec directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
